summary.intent: Resolve 8 P2 findings from Codex's second-pass review of a Rust color picker component, fixing 7 clear bugs and making a design decision on a spec self-contradiction about how the 2D color area maps to channels across color spaces.
summary.outcome: All 8 findings resolved: 7 bug fixes implemented and Option 3 (HSB-aware area axis: sat×lightness for HSL/RGB/HEX, sat×brightness for HSB) adopted to reconcile the spec contradiction. Spec updated end-to-end, 91 tests pass, coverage gate holds at 90.1% branch, committed and pushed.

repo: The spec had a genuine self-contradiction: §1.6 step 3 described full per-space channel remapping (including RGB), while DragTarget::Area anatomy declared the area fixed to saturation/lightness — these cannot both be true.
Real color pickers (Ark UI, React Aria) never implement a per-RGB-channel 2D area; RGB/HEX reuse the HSL square as the visual picker since they have no hue square.
The open field in Context was previously Option<bool> (controlled slot pattern) and was simplified to plain bool — uncontrolled open simply retains current state and responds to user toggles.
channels() was omitting the alpha channel entirely, and HSL value_as_string was leaking alpha even when show_alpha=false.
Hue endpoint collapse (0°=360° discontinuity) required storing an unwrapped hue_value: f32 separately in Context rather than reading hue back from the color value on each operation.
Swatch elements were missing type="button", which is required to prevent accidental form submission.

code.finding: Context struct: open changed from Option<bool> to bool; hue_value: f32 added to track unwrapped hue and avoid endpoint collapse.
area_y_channel() helper returns 'lightness' for HSL/RGB/HEX and 'brightness' for HSB, driving the area thumb y-axis label and apply_drag_position mapping.
DragEnd was missing from the disabled guard, allowing drag-end events to mutate state while the picker was disabled.
channel_current() helper added; EyedropperResult, SyncValue, and SetColor all need to sync hue_value after any color change to keep the unwrapped hue consistent.

§1.6 step 3 reconciled to Option 3 design: area is always sat×{lightness|brightness} depending on color space; §1.3 Context, §1.5, §1.7 pseudocode, §1.8 highlights, §2 anatomy AreaThumb/Swatch rows, and §4.1 Messages all updated.

workflow: Codex review workflow: poll background task for verdict, fetch unresolved threads, categorize as bugs vs design decisions, fix bugs first, escalate design forks to user before implementing.
Coverage gate run as a background task after tests/clippy/insta pass; commit and push gated on coverage result.
Insta snapshot tests (Rust insta crate) require explicit re-acceptance when intentional output changes occur (e.g., adding type="button" to swatch snapshots).
Large single-file components require many sequential read-edit cycles; structuring edits by region (Context → helpers → transition arms → connect-side methods → tests → spec) reduces re-reads.

friction: Spec self-contradiction required pausing implementation to present options to the user — could not be resolved unilaterally.
A const fn clippy warning emerged only after the main implementation was complete, requiring an extra fix pass.
The color picker is a single large file; navigating it required many sequential Bash grep + Read + Edit cycles with no way to batch the edits.
open_items: Reply to and resolve all 8 Codex PR threads with rationale for each fix.
Re-trigger @codex review after threads are resolved to get a third-pass confirmation.
HWB color space handling in channels() was noted but not fully addressed (comment added as a note).
